title: Hub-Spoke Lug Sync -- cross-spoke lug distribution protocol
what: The hub-spoke sync protocol distributes lugs between spokes using outgoing/incoming directories as the transport layer. A spoke places lugs in WAI-Spoke/lugs/outgoing/ for the hub to pick up. The hub distributes them to target spokes' incoming/ directories. Delivery confirmations flow back. Signals use the faster signal channel.
why: Without hub-spoke sync, framework improvements require direct filesystem access to each spoke - which is not possible across machines. The outgoing/incoming transport layer decouples delivery from direct access.

use_cases.title: Distributing a FRAMEWORK impl lug
use_cases.trigger: Framework spoke creates an impl lug with routed_to=FRAMEWORK
use_cases.outcome: At closeout, lug copied to outgoing/. Hub picks it up and distributes to each active spoke's incoming/. Each spoke adopts at next session.

use_cases.title: Teaching distribution
use_cases.outcome: Teaching distributed to all spoke seed/ingest/ directories via transport. Each spoke adopts at next session start.

patterns.name: Outgoing is fire-and-forget
patterns.description: The spoke places lugs in outgoing/ and closes out. Hub pickup is asynchronous - the spoke does not wait for acknowledgment.

patterns.name: FRAMEWORK vs SIGNAL routing
patterns.description: FRAMEWORK routes work that can queue for normal dispatch. SIGNAL routes urgent patches applied at next session start. Never conflate them.

how.trigger: Closeout: any lug with routed_to=FRAMEWORK or SIGNAL is copied to outgoing/
steps_summary: 1. At closeout: scan bytype/ for lugs with routed_to=FRAMEWORK or SIGNAL
3. Hub gardener picks up outgoing/ contents at next hub session
4. Hub distributes to each spoke's incoming/ per routing target
5. Spoke wakeup reads incoming/, acknowledges, processes lug
constraints: Never modify a lug in outgoing/ after placement - may already be in transit
Delivery confirmation must be sent back to source spoke on receipt

triggers: Manual outgoing/ placement for urgent items
when.frequency: Each closeout with FRAMEWORK or SIGNAL lugs

inputs: bytype/ lugs with routed_to=FRAMEWORK or SIGNAL

constraints: FRAMEWORK lugs are for all spokes - never route spoke-specific work as FRAMEWORK
body: The hub-spoke sync protocol moves lugs and teachings between spokes without requiring direct filesystem access across machines. The transport mechanism is a symmetric outgoing/incoming directory pair: a spoke writes to WAI-Spoke/lugs/outgoing/, the hub gardener reads it and copies to each target spoke's WAI-Spoke/lugs/incoming/. The spoke wakeup protocol processes incoming/ at session start. Delivery is asynchronous — the originating spoke does not wait for acknowledgment and closes out immediately after writing to outgoing/. Three routing values trigger outgoing placement: FRAMEWORK (improvement relevant to all spokes), SPOKE/{spoke_id} (work for a specific spoke), and ASSESSOR (model telemetry deposited to the hub assessor inbox). The routing value SIGNAL is fully deprecated — no new lugs should use it. Cross-spoke lugs must pass a pre-delivery checklist before placement: perceive, execute, and verify must be non-empty; destination_wheel_id must be resolvable in hub-registry.json; acceptance_criteria must be non-empty; effort_score and model_fit must be set. After checklist passes, the lug is written to outgoing/ and simultaneously copied to the target spoke's incoming/ via hub-registry.json path lookup. Teaching distribution follows the same transport layer but uses the seed/ingest/ target directory on each spoke rather than lugs/incoming/. Delivery confirmations flow back as delivery_confirmation lugs in the originating spoke's incoming/ directory.
done_when: A lug with routed_to=FRAMEWORK placed in outgoing/ at closeout is present in the target spoke's WAI-Spoke/lugs/incoming/ after the hub gardener run
hub-registry.json lookup resolves a wheel_id to the correct spoke filesystem path for delivery
The pre-delivery checklist rejects a lug with empty perceive and does not copy it to outgoing/
A delivery_confirmation lug is written to the originating spoke's incoming/ after the target spoke acknowledges receipt
Lugs with routed_to=SPOKE/{spoke_id} land in that specific spoke's incoming/ and not in any other spoke's incoming/
